rulings.Case Title: JESSICA JIMENEZ VS. MENZIES AVIATION, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Responsive Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Of At Least $3,680 Against Defendants
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 4, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JESSICA JIMENEZ Hearing Plaintiffs' Motion To Compel Defendants To Provide Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Responsive Documents, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Of At Least $3,680 Against Defendants. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to November 20, 2014 to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the moving papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than November 4, 2014 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FELIX G FLORES VS. EXECUPARK, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Jose Zelaya'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Felix G. Calle Flores' Second Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: mATTER ON CALENDAR ON tUESDAY, nOVEMBER 4, 2014, lINE 6, DEFENDANT JOSE ZELAYA'S Motion To Strike Portions Of Plaintiff Felix G. Calle Flores' Second Amended Complaint. Granted. The third cause of action for Intentional Tort is stricken. Plaintiff did not obtain leave of court to add a cause of action to the complaint. The exemplary damages attachment is also stricken, as a negligence claim cannot support a prayer of punitive damages. If plaintiff wants to add a claim for Intentional Tort, plaintiff must file a motion seeking leave of court. (See CCP sec. 472, providing that any pleading may be amended only once without leave of court.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: HEATHER RODRIGUES et al VS. PRINCIPAL BUILDERS INC A CALIFORNIOA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order Preventing Defts From Depositing Pltff'S Counsel Ashley Baltazar And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Deft And Its Attorney Of Record
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, November 4, 2014, Line 14, PLAINTIFFs HEATHER RODRIGUES, STACY BALL-WEAVER and LAUARIE ANDESON's Motion For Protective Order Preventing Defendants From Depositing Plaintiff'S Counsel Ashley Baltazar And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant And Its Attorney Of Record. Granted. Defendants have not established the "extremely good cause" required to allow for the deposition of opposing counsel. (Carehouse Convalescent Hospital v. Sup. Ct. (2006) 143 Cal.App.4th 1558, 1562.) Defendants shall pay Plaintiffs sanctions in the amount of $3,340 within 30 days of entry of this Order. (CCP sec. 2025.420(h).) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CARMEN DIAZ ECHAURI VS. EKO VENTURE, INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Deft'S Further Response To Req.S For Production, Set Two And Req. For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 4, 2014, Line 2, PLAINTIFF CARMEN ECHAURI Amended Motion To Compel Defendant's Further Response To Requests For Production, Set Two And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motion to compel production of the "timeline" document utilized by a witness to refresh his recollection at deposition is denied. The court may not order production of the document for in camera review. (Costco Wholesale Corp. v. Superior Court, 47 Cal. 4th 725, 739 (2009) It accepts the declaration from counsel the subject document contains attorney opinions, impressions, and notes of meetings. Therefore it is protected attorney work product. Absent waiver an attorney work product document is absolutely privileged against discovery. (BP Alaska Exploration vs. Superior Court 199 Cal. App. 3d 1240 (1988). The mere showing of an absolutely privileged document to a represented witness in preparation for deposition testimony does not automatically result in the document's loss of its privileged protected status. (Sullivan vs. Superior Court, 29 Cal. App. 3d 64 (1972). At the deposition defense counsel immediately objected to deposition questioning as to the document invoking the privilege. (Evidence Code 912(a). There was no waiver. The request for monetary sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT L. LURIE VS. ROBERT A. LURIE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, November 4, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANTs H. KURZMAN, EUGENE VALLA, JAMES HUNT and WILLIAM HARRIS' DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend. (CCP sec. 430.10(e).) As to the second cause of action for fraudulent concealment and the third cause of action for breach of fiduciary duty, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing a duty owed by Defendants that does not arise from contract. As to the fourth cause of action for intentional interference with contractual relations, no cause of action is stated, as Defendants were also parties to the Agreement. (See Applied Equip. Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabi Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 514.) As to the fifth cause of action for accounting, Plaintiff is given leave to amend to allege facts establishing that he does not have an adequate legal remedy and that the amount of damages is not ascertainable. (See Prakashpalan v. Engstrom, Lipscomb and Lack (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1105, 1136-37.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GREGG M. WILCOX VS. ROBERT S. SALK DPM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (First Appearance Fee Paid On)Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Verified Complaint And Request For Attorneys Fees
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Tuesday, November 4, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT ROBERT SALK's Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Verified Complaint And Request For Attorneys Fees. The Court grants the anti-SLAPP motion to strike. Defendant's statements to the police and District Attorney trigger the first prong of CCP 425.16. See Salma v. Capon (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 1275, 1285. Section 425.16, subdivision (e) defines an " 'act in furtherance of a person's right of petition ...' " to include: "(1) any written or oral statement or writing made before a legislative, executive, or judicial proceeding, or any other official proceeding authorized by law; (2) any written or oral statement or writing made in connection with an issue under consideration or review by a legislative, executive, or judicial body, or any other official proceeding authorized by law ...." (§ 425.16, subd. (e).) Defendant's communications made in preparation for or in anticipation of the bringing of an action or other official proceeding fall within the ambit of these subdivisions. Plaintiff's reliance on Flately v. Muro (2006) 39 Cal.4th 299, is misplaced. The criminal exception to the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis applies where the criminal conduct is conceded or conclusively established. In addition, the mere violation of a statute does not meet the criminal illegality requirement. See Fremont Reorgnizing Corp. v. Faigin (2011) 198 Cal.App.4th 1153, 1169. Here, defendant's conduct was privileged and not criminal. See Shaddox v. Bertani (2003) 110 Cal.App.4th 1406, 1415-1417. The Court denies the request for a continuance. Plaintiff fails to explain how the deposition will change the analysis. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
